title: High Schoolers Just Built a Blanket Fort So Big It Beat a World Record
script: High school students in Las Vegas just built a blanket fort so massive it officially broke a Guinness World Record. The fort stretched across 14,103 square feet at a local community center — larger than most houses. Students from West Career and Technical Academy designed it all, using donated sheets and scaffolding. Guinness rules: no gaps bigger than 1 inch, sheets must touch the floor, and people must be able to sit inside. Previous record: 12,291 square feet. Gone.
